unit: W8 — README rewrite + install-scope guidance per W0 V1 finding

contract.goal: Rewrite README.md to document the plugin-install flow (replacing the old install.sh symlink-farm narrative). Adopt the W0 V1 install-scope refinement: recommend `claude plugin install agent-loop-full@krambuhl --scope user` as the primary install; document `--scope local` as a per-project variant explicitly requiring `.claude/settings.local.json` in the consumer repo's `.gitignore` as a load-bearing mitigation.
acceptance_criteria: README.md install section names `claude plugin install agent-loop-full@krambuhl --scope user` as the primary recommended install command.
README.md documents the `--scope user` vs `--scope local` trade-off, names the gitignore footgun explicitly, and cites V1 as the evidence basis.
README.md lists all 6 plugins + their dependencies in a table.
README.md mentions `griot init` as the post-install per-project step for projects that want a project-local learnings tree.
README.md describes the canonical-vs-generated layout (top-level cli/ + skills/ + agents/ as source-of-truth; plugins/<name>/ as mirrored).
Old skills-CLI section (npx skills add) removed per PLAN § Out.
npm test green (README changes don't affect tests).
rules_applied: Adopt W0 V1 finding: --scope user as primary; --scope local as per-project variant with explicit gitignore caveat.
Migration PR conventions per CLAUDE.md: ## Summary with bullet points, table of files changed (this checkin's notes_for_pr).
Remove dropped scope: npx skills add compatibility (PLAN § Out).
disqualifiers: No grep tripwire for install.sh refs (W9 owns).
No content changes to plugin sources, sync script, or skill bodies.
inputs: projects/2026-05-19-marketplace-portable-install/PLAN.md § Scope (in) install instructions, § Verification V1, § R4, § Decisions (primary install scope = --scope user, revision 3)
Current README.md (85 lines; install.sh + bin/loom adopt narrative)
Claude Code docs (plugin-marketplaces, plugin-dependencies) — already-cited in this session's prior workstreams

actions: Read current README.md (85 lines): scope was install.sh-driven symlink-farm; `bin/loom adopt` per-project flow; npx skills add compatibility section.

Wrote new README.md (145 lines). Structure: ## Install (primary --scope user + granular variant + scopes table); ## After install: per-project griot init; ## What's inside (table covering marketplace.json + plugins/ + canonical cli/skills/agents/ + scripts/sync-shared.ts + learnings/ + docs/); ## Authoring against this marketplace (canonical-vs-generated explanation + sync-shared workflow); ## Where this came from (genesis + migration history).

Install-scope guidance adopted from W0 V1: primary command `claude plugin install agent-loop-full@krambuhl --scope user`. Granular variant documented with the same scope. `--scope local` documented as per-project variant with the load-bearing `.gitignore` requirement called out explicitly + the V1 finding cited as evidence basis.
Removed: install.sh narrative, ~/.agents symlink-farm description, bin/loom adopt per-project flow, npx skills add compatibility section.

Added: 6-plugin table with cascade dependencies; install-scope rationale with the colleague-invisibility constraint named; griot init post-install step; canonical-vs-generated layout explanation; sync-shared.ts authoring workflow.
npm test → 608/608 still passing (README is documentation; no test impact).

changes_since_previous: W7 (e00c808) landed the sync-shared.ts + first sync run. With plugins/<plugin>/cli/ now populated, the marketplace is minimum-viable installable end-to-end. W8 rewrites README to document the new install flow + adopt the W0 V1 --scope user finding.
notes_for_pr: Install-scope guidance: README primary command is `claude plugin install agent-loop-full@krambuhl --scope user`. This adopts the W0 V1 finding (PLAN revision 3) — `--scope local` was PLAN revision 1's default, but V1 established that a checked-in .claude/settings.local.json IS honored on colleague clones at local-settings precedence (the file is gitignored by Claude Code convention, not enforcement). --scope user writes ~/.claude/settings.json and never lands in any repo, so the 'invisible to colleagues' constraint is satisfied by construction.

--scope local is documented as a per-project variant for users who want it — but the README calls out the load-bearing .gitignore requirement explicitly. V1 is cited as the evidence basis so future maintainers (or AI agents) reading the README can trace the rationale back through the migration's checkin history.

Removed: install.sh + ~/.agents symlink-farm narrative (W9 deletes the script itself), bin/loom adopt per-project flow (no longer needed — bin shims are inside the plugin tree), npx skills add compatibility section (dropped per PLAN § Out: 'undocumented in official Claude Code docs').

Added: 6-plugin table with cascade dependencies (loom → guild+griot; ev → loom+guild+griot; agent-loop-full → all five), griot init post-install step explanation, canonical-vs-generated layout explanation pointing at scripts/sync-shared.ts as the canonical authoring workflow.

V6/V7 (full install smoke tests post-merge) remain the empirical ground-truth check on the README's install instructions. If V7 (Patreon machine) contradicts the documented colleague-invisibility claim, the README's --scope user recommendation would need a follow-up revision — but the documented-behavior reasoning in W0 V1 + the migration's broader testing surface (V9, V10, V11) make a contradiction unlikely.
